title: Grade 5 Math
description: STAAR Grade 5 Math curriculum aligned to the Texas Essential Knowledge and Skills (TEKS). Topics build from decimal place value through volume, data, and personal financial literacy.

units.title: Place Value with Decimals
units.summary: Read, write, compare, and round decimals to the thousandths.

lessons.title: Decimal place value to thousandths
lessons.objective: Represent the value of a digit in decimals through thousandths.

questions.prompt: What is the value of the 7 in 4.187?
questions.explanation: The 7 is in the third position after the decimal point, the thousandths place.

questions.prompt: What digit is in the thousandths place of 919.413?
questions.explanation: Tenths is the 1st digit after the decimal, hundredths the 2nd, thousandths the 3rd. In 919.413 the thousandths digit is 3.

questions.prompt: What digit is in the thousandths place of 952.289?
questions.explanation: Tenths is the 1st digit after the decimal, hundredths the 2nd, thousandths the 3rd. In 952.289 the thousandths digit is 9.

units.title: Multiplying & Dividing Decimals
units.summary: Multiply and divide decimals by whole numbers and other decimals.

lessons.title: Multiplying decimals by whole numbers
lessons.objective: Multiply a decimal by a whole number.

questions.explanation: 6 × 7 = 42, then place one decimal: 4.2.
Multiply ignoring the decimal: 97 × 5 = 485, then place 1 decimal: 48.5.

lessons.objective: Divide decimals by whole numbers.

units.title: Adding & Subtracting Fractions (Unlike Denominators)
units.summary: Add and subtract fractions and mixed numbers with unlike denominators.

lessons.title: Adding unlike fractions
lessons.objective: Add fractions with unlike denominators by finding a common denominator.

questions.prompt: 2/3 + 3/6 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 6. Convert: 2/3 = 4/6, 3/6 = 3/6. Sum = 7/6 = 7/6.

questions.prompt: 1/3 + 1/5 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 15. Convert: 1/3 = 5/15, 1/5 = 3/15. Sum = 8/15 = 8/15.

questions.prompt: 1/3 + 1/2 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 6. Convert: 1/3 = 2/6, 1/2 = 3/6. Sum = 5/6 = 5/6.

questions.prompt: 2/5 + 2/8 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 40. Convert: 2/5 = 16/40, 2/8 = 10/40. Sum = 26/40 = 13/20.

questions.prompt: 2/3 + 4/12 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 12. Convert: 2/3 = 8/12, 4/12 = 4/12. Sum = 12/12 = 1.

questions.prompt: 1/8 + 5/12 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 24. Convert: 1/8 = 3/24, 5/12 = 10/24. Sum = 13/24 = 13/24.

questions.prompt: 3/5 + 3/4 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 20. Convert: 3/5 = 12/20, 3/4 = 15/20. Sum = 27/20 = 27/20.

questions.prompt: 1/5 + 1/2 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 10. Convert: 1/5 = 2/10, 1/2 = 5/10. Sum = 7/10 = 7/10.

questions.prompt: 3/6 + 6/8 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 24. Convert: 3/6 = 12/24, 6/8 = 18/24. Sum = 30/24 = 5/4.

questions.prompt: 1/5 + 2/6 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 30. Convert: 1/5 = 6/30, 2/6 = 10/30. Sum = 16/30 = 8/15.

questions.prompt: 3/8 + 1/5 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 40. Convert: 3/8 = 15/40, 1/5 = 8/40. Sum = 23/40 = 23/40.

questions.prompt: 2/8 + 2/6 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 24. Convert: 2/8 = 6/24, 2/6 = 8/24. Sum = 14/24 = 7/12.

questions.prompt: 1/3 + 10/12 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 12. Convert: 1/3 = 4/12, 10/12 = 10/12. Sum = 14/12 = 7/6.

questions.prompt: 3/5 + 1/6 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 30. Convert: 3/5 = 18/30, 1/6 = 5/30. Sum = 23/30 = 23/30.

questions.prompt: 1/4 + 9/10 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 20. Convert: 1/4 = 5/20, 9/10 = 18/20. Sum = 23/20 = 23/20.

questions.prompt: 1/4 + 4/8 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 8. Convert: 1/4 = 2/8, 4/8 = 4/8. Sum = 6/8 = 3/4.

questions.prompt: 3/4 + 1/6 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 12. Convert: 3/4 = 9/12, 1/6 = 2/12. Sum = 11/12 = 11/12.

questions.prompt: 1/4 + 1/3 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 12. Convert: 1/4 = 3/12, 1/3 = 4/12. Sum = 7/12 = 7/12.

questions.prompt: 3/8 + 1/2 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 8. Convert: 3/8 = 3/8, 1/2 = 4/8. Sum = 7/8 = 7/8.

questions.prompt: 1/8 + 4/5 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 40. Convert: 1/8 = 5/40, 4/5 = 32/40. Sum = 37/40 = 37/40.

questions.prompt: 1/6 + 5/8 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 24. Convert: 1/6 = 4/24, 5/8 = 15/24. Sum = 19/24 = 19/24.

questions.prompt: 3/4 + 1/3 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 12. Convert: 3/4 = 9/12, 1/3 = 4/12. Sum = 13/12 = 13/12.

questions.prompt: 6/8 + 1/3 = ?  (give answer in simplest form, like 3/4 or 1)
questions.explanation: Common denominator is 24. Convert: 6/8 = 18/24, 1/3 = 8/24. Sum = 26/24 = 13/12.

lessons.title: Subtracting unlike fractions
lessons.objective: Subtract fractions with unlike denominators.

questions.explanation: Common denominator 12: 3/4 = 9/12, 2/3 = 8/12. Difference = 1/12 = 1/12.
Common denominator 6: 2/3 = 4/6, 1/2 = 3/6. Difference = 1/6 = 1/6.
Common denominator 12: 3/6 = 6/12, 1/4 = 3/12. Difference = 3/12 = 1/4.
Common denominator 12: 3/4 = 9/12, 7/12 = 7/12. Difference = 2/12 = 1/6.
Common denominator 30: 8/10 = 24/30, 3/6 = 15/30. Difference = 9/30 = 3/10.
Common denominator 15: 1/3 = 5/15, 1/5 = 3/15. Difference = 2/15 = 2/15.
Common denominator 30: 3/5 = 18/30, 3/6 = 15/30. Difference = 3/30 = 1/10.
Common denominator 12: 11/12 = 11/12, 2/4 = 6/12. Difference = 5/12 = 5/12.
Common denominator 4: 1/2 = 2/4, 1/4 = 1/4. Difference = 1/4 = 1/4.
Common denominator 20: 3/5 = 12/20, 2/4 = 10/20. Difference = 2/20 = 1/10.
Common denominator 24: 3/6 = 12/24, 3/8 = 9/24. Difference = 3/24 = 1/8.
Common denominator 30: 1/3 = 10/30, 3/10 = 9/30. Difference = 1/30 = 1/30.
Common denominator 30: 4/5 = 24/30, 2/6 = 10/30. Difference = 14/30 = 7/15.
Common denominator 8: 5/8 = 5/8, 1/2 = 4/8. Difference = 1/8 = 1/8.
Common denominator 24: 7/8 = 21/24, 2/3 = 16/24. Difference = 5/24 = 5/24.
Common denominator 24: 5/12 = 10/24, 2/8 = 6/24. Difference = 4/24 = 1/6.
Common denominator 24: 1/3 = 8/24, 1/8 = 3/24. Difference = 5/24 = 5/24.
Common denominator 10: 3/5 = 6/10, 1/2 = 5/10. Difference = 1/10 = 1/10.
Common denominator 8: 2/4 = 4/8, 2/8 = 2/8. Difference = 2/8 = 1/4.
Common denominator 20: 3/4 = 15/20, 1/5 = 4/20. Difference = 11/20 = 11/20.
Common denominator 10: 1/2 = 5/10, 1/5 = 2/10. Difference = 3/10 = 3/10.
Common denominator 40: 5/8 = 25/40, 1/5 = 8/40. Difference = 17/40 = 17/40.
Common denominator 30: 6/10 = 18/30, 1/3 = 10/30. Difference = 8/30 = 4/15.
Common denominator 60: 10/12 = 50/60, 2/5 = 24/60. Difference = 26/60 = 13/30.

units.title: Multiplying & Dividing Fractions
units.summary: Multiply a whole number by a fraction and divide unit fractions by whole numbers (and the reverse).

lessons.title: Whole number × fraction
lessons.objective: Represent and solve multiplication of a whole number by a fraction.

lessons.title: Unit fraction ÷ whole number
lessons.objective: Divide a unit fraction by a whole number.

questions.explanation: Splitting 1/4 into 2 equal parts gives 1/8 each.

lessons.title: Whole number ÷ unit fraction
lessons.objective: Divide a whole number by a unit fraction.

questions.prompt: How many 1/3-cup servings are in 4 cups?

units.title: Order of Operations & Patterns
units.summary: Use order of operations and identify rules in numerical patterns.

lessons.title: Order of operations
lessons.objective: Simplify expressions using order of operations (no exponents).

questions.explanation: Parentheses: 6−2 = 4. Multiply: 4×4 = 16. Add: 12+16 = 28.
Multiply first: 2 × 3 = 6. Then 5 + 6 − 2 = 9.
Multiply first: 7 × 3 = 21. Then 2 + 21 − 3 = 20.
Multiply first: 7 × 8 = 56. Then 3 + 56 − 4 = 55.
Multiply first: 8 × 6 = 48. Then 6 + 48 − 3 = 51.
Multiply first: 6 × 4 = 24. Then 8 + 24 − 5 = 27.
Multiply first: 9 × 7 = 63. Then 5 + 63 − 3 = 65.
Multiply first: 7 × 8 = 56. Then 6 + 56 − 4 = 58.
Multiply first: 9 × 3 = 27. Then 4 + 27 − 4 = 27.
Multiply first: 8 × 7 = 56. Then 5 + 56 − 5 = 56.
Multiply first: 6 × 6 = 36. Then 2 + 36 − 5 = 33.
Multiply first: 6 × 2 = 12. Then 8 + 12 − 5 = 15.
Multiply first: 7 × 3 = 21. Then 9 + 21 − 5 = 25.
Multiply first: 5 × 3 = 15. Then 5 + 15 − 3 = 17.
Multiply first: 8 × 7 = 56. Then 7 + 56 − 5 = 58.
Multiply first: 6 × 3 = 18. Then 4 + 18 − 3 = 19.
Multiply first: 4 × 6 = 24. Then 7 + 24 − 5 = 26.
Multiply first: 7 × 4 = 28. Then 6 + 28 − 5 = 29.
Multiply first: 6 × 4 = 24. Then 4 + 24 − 3 = 25.
Multiply first: 2 × 7 = 14. Then 3 + 14 − 5 = 12.
Multiply first: 3 × 9 = 27. Then 5 + 27 − 4 = 28.
Multiply first: 5 × 7 = 35. Then 9 + 35 − 3 = 41.
Multiply first: 6 × 6 = 36. Then 2 + 36 − 3 = 35.
Multiply first: 9 × 4 = 36. Then 7 + 36 − 3 = 40.
Multiply first: 9 × 6 = 54. Then 2 + 54 − 5 = 51.

lessons.title: Numerical patterns
lessons.objective: Generate a numerical pattern given a rule.

questions.prompt: If the rule is 'multiply by 2 and add 3', what comes after 5?

units.title: Coordinate Plane
units.summary: Plot ordered pairs in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane.

lessons.title: Plotting ordered pairs
lessons.objective: Identify and plot points using ordered pairs.

questions.prompt: Which ordered pair is 4 units right and 2 units up from the origin?
questions.explanation: Ordered pairs are (x, y), so 4 right then 2 up is (4, 2).

units.title: Classifying 2D Figures
units.summary: Classify two-dimensional figures into categories based on hierarchical attributes.

lessons.title: Quadrilateral hierarchy
lessons.objective: Classify quadrilaterals based on attributes.

questions.explanation: A square is a special rectangle (4 right angles, opposite sides parallel) with all sides equal.

units.title: Volume of Rectangular Prisms
units.summary: Find volume of rectangular prisms using V = l × w × h.

lessons.title: Volume formula
lessons.objective: Apply V = l × w × h to find volume.

questions.prompt: A box has length 5 cm, width 3 cm, and height 4 cm. What is its volume in cubic cm?
questions.explanation: V = 5 × 3 × 4 = 60 cubic cm.

questions.prompt: Find the volume of a rectangular prism that is 12 × 2 × 4 units.
questions.explanation: V = l × w × h = 12 × 2 × 4 = 96 cubic units.

questions.prompt: Find the volume of a rectangular prism that is 8 × 11 × 10 units.
questions.explanation: V = l × w × h = 8 × 11 × 10 = 880 cubic units.

questions.explanation: V = l × w × h = 4 × 5 × 10 = 200 cubic units.

questions.explanation: V = l × w × h = 7 × 4 × 6 = 168 cubic units.

questions.explanation: V = l × w × h = 6 × 10 × 3 = 180 cubic units.

questions.explanation: V = l × w × h = 11 × 12 × 5 = 660 cubic units.

questions.explanation: V = l × w × h = 12 × 10 × 2 = 240 cubic units.

questions.explanation: V = l × w × h = 11 × 8 × 4 = 352 cubic units.

lessons.title: Counting unit cubes
lessons.objective: Find volume by counting unit cubes.

questions.prompt: A prism is filled with 2 layers of unit cubes. Each layer is 4 by 3. What is the total volume?
questions.explanation: Each layer has 4 × 3 = 12 cubes, and there are 2 layers: 12 × 2 = 24.

units.title: Measurement Conversions
units.summary: Solve problems by converting within customary or metric measurement systems.

lessons.title: Customary conversions
lessons.objective: Convert customary units of length, weight, and capacity.

questions.prompt: How many ounces are in 3 pounds?
questions.explanation: 1 pound = 16 ounces, so 3 × 16 = 48 oz.
1 feet = 12 inches, so 8 feet = 96 inches.
1 quart = 2 pints, so 3 quarts = 6 pints.
1 quart = 2 pints, so 6 quarts = 12 pints.
1 gallon = 4 quarts, so 3 gallons = 12 quarts.
1 quart = 2 pints, so 10 quarts = 20 pints.
1 feet = 12 inches, so 5 feet = 60 inches.
1 gallon = 4 quarts, so 12 gallons = 48 quarts.
1 gallon = 4 quarts, so 5 gallons = 20 quarts.
1 feet = 12 inches, so 4 feet = 48 inches.
1 yard = 3 feet, so 10 yards = 30 feet.
1 pound = 16 ounces, so 12 pounds = 192 ounces.
1 yard = 3 feet, so 11 yards = 33 feet.
1 yard = 3 feet, so 9 yards = 27 feet.
1 pound = 16 ounces, so 11 pounds = 176 ounces.
1 quart = 2 pints, so 4 quarts = 8 pints.
1 gallon = 4 quarts, so 11 gallons = 44 quarts.
1 gallon = 4 quarts, so 9 gallons = 36 quarts.
1 feet = 12 inches, so 11 feet = 132 inches.
1 yard = 3 feet, so 4 yards = 12 feet.

lessons.title: Metric conversions
lessons.objective: Convert metric units of length and capacity.

questions.prompt: How many milliliters are in 2.5 liters?

questions.explanation: 1 m = 100 cm, so 9 m = 900 cm.

units.title: Data Analysis
units.reportingCategory: Data Analysis and Personal Financial Literacy
units.summary: Represent and interpret data with bar graphs, dot plots, scatterplots, and stem-and-leaf plots.

lessons.title: Reading scatterplots
lessons.objective: Interpret discrete data presented in a scatterplot.

questions.prompt: A scatterplot shows hours studied (x) vs. test score (y). Most points go up to the right. This pattern suggests…
choices: More study time tends to mean lower scores.
More study time tends to mean higher scores.
questions.answer: More study time tends to mean higher scores.
questions.explanation: An upward pattern (positive trend) means y increases as x increases.

units.title: Personal Financial Literacy
units.reportingCategory: Data Analysis and Personal Financial Literacy
units.summary: Identify income types, taxes, balance a simple budget, and weigh the cost of borrowing.

lessons.title: Gross vs net income
lessons.objective: Define gross income and net income.

questions.prompt: Lily earns $400 a week. After taxes, she takes home $340. The $340 is her…
questions.explanation: Net income is what remains after taxes are subtracted from gross income.

lessons.title: Simple budget
lessons.objective: Balance a simple budget by comparing income and expenses.

questions.prompt: Income is $250 and expenses are $215. How many dollars are left to save?
